name: DOGE 4h Value Recovery Scale-In v1
edge_mechanism: price flushes away from short-term value, reclaims value, confirmation improves across bars, and exposure is built in stages instead of all at once
thesis: The last five cycles say sparse continuation theater is dead and pure management experiments on weak triggers are worse. DOGE 4h remains the best live lane for a denser recovery mechanism because it is transition-heavy enough to create repeated flush-and-reclaim sequences without forcing anti-trend ETH chop or late TAO continuation. The edge is staged participation only after recovery proves itself.
invalidation_condition: If DOGE 4h still cannot produce enough recovery sequences to clear density, or if staged adds mostly build into failed bounces, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes below short-term value, reclaims it, improves confirmation on subsequent bars, and resumes away from the recovery zone.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition markets often punish one-shot bounce timing but reward adding into improving confirmation. This is a denser, more tradeable expression than rare delayed-retest continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is the best fit because it is the live primary lane, sits in transition, and should express recovery/reclaim behavior more often than TAO trend continuation or ETH flat chop.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Staged entries can still average into weak mean-reversion bounces if the regime slips back into chop without follow-through.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is healthy but expectancy is weak, cut second-add size and bring the first partial closer before changing the trigger grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Repeated recovery attempts, many modest first-target wins, and fewer larger moves when recovery becomes true continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: DOGE 4h clears density and staged recovery materially outperforms one-shot recovery timing.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated scale-ins that behave like averaging losers.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because flush-and-recovery sequences recur in transition

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit transition lane for staged value recovery.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE 4h shows enough value-recovery sequences with scale-in improving net expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h staged entries still mostly build into failed bounces.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with several recovery continuations.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same staged recovery logic survives on a flatter similar lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h retains a smaller but tradable staged recovery behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h is too flat or too shallow to monetize.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner, smaller recovery continuations than DOGE.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same recovery grammar survives trend contamination without becoming late continuation buying.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h retains enough recovery structure with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h confirms the concept is too transition-specific.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, stronger recovered continuation than DOGE.

long: Initial 60% size on first close back above VWAP
Add 40% only if next bar closes >= EMA_20 and low stays >= VWAP - 0.07 * ATR_14
short: Initial 60% size on first close back below VWAP
Add 40% only if next bar closes <= EMA_20 and high stays <= VWAP + 0.07 * ATR_14

long: Stop loss: 0.80 * ATR_14 below blended entry
short: Stop loss: 0.80 * ATR_14 above blended entry
